_comment: Home page copy — every heading, paragraph, button, card, and image on the home page (src/pages/index.astro). Edit here or via the CMS; the page layout and styles stay in the .astro file. The map pins come from the Branches collection, not this file.

seo.title: Sakyadhita · International Association of Buddhist Women
seo.description: Since 1987, Sakyadhita has brought together laywomen, monastics, and scholars from across all Buddhist traditions to advance equality, education, and spiritual practice.

hero.announcement: Celebrating our 20th biennial international conference in 2027
hero.text: Since 1987, we have been bringing together laywomen, monastics, and scholars from across all Buddhist traditions to advance equality, education, and spiritual practice.

mission.heading: Three pillars of our work
mission.intro: Sakyadhita is a grassroots network connecting Buddhist women worldwide. We promote research on Buddhist women's history and work for equal opportunity across all Buddhist traditions. Through local branches, free online resources, and our biannual conferences, we help empower the world's 300 million Buddhist women to work for peace and social justice.

pillars.title: Connect
pillars.text: A communications network linking some 300 million Buddhist women worldwide through branches, chapters, and online community.

pillars.title: Advance Equity
pillars.text: Working for gender equity in Buddhist education, training, institutional structures, and full ordination across traditions.

pillars.title: Convene
pillars.text: Hosting the world's largest gathering of Buddhist women — our biennial international conference, now in its 19th edition.

featuredConference.heading: 20th International Conference
featuredConference.sub: Celebrating our 20th biennial international conference in 2027

card.title: 20th Sakyadhita International Conference on Buddhist Women
card.text: In 2027, the 20th Sakyadhita International Conference travels to Indonesia, bringing together Buddhist women from across the world's traditions for teachings, scholarship, and friendship.
card.cfpNote: The Academic Program Committee is now receiving paper proposals for the 20th Sakyadhita International Conference 2027. Paper proposals should be submitted by July 30, 2026.

highlights.heading: Highlights of the 19th Sakyadhita Conference in Sarawak, East Malaysia.
highlights.caption: SARAWAK · JUNE 2025

cards.title: Navigating Change: Buddhist Women in Transition

feature.title: Volume 33, Number 1
feature.text: Reflections from the 18th Conference in Seoul, and a preview of Sarawak 2025.

items.title: 265 nuns receive full ordination in Bhutan

branches.heading: Find Sakyadhita near you
branches.text:
National branches and local chapters organize events, run translation projects, and bring Sakyadhita's mission to communities worldwide.
Click a pin to find your nearest branch.

cards.title: Join Us
cards.text: Become a member of our global network. Member tiers start at $15 USD.

cards.title: Volunteer
cards.text: 15 committees need help — from translation to conference planning.

cards.title: Donate
cards.text: Fund conference scholarships, ordination advocacy, and the newsletter.
